notes: Cannot play on EAC enabled servers at least (Couldn't test EAC disabled as there were none). Game launched after putting  cd "/<YOUR STEAM DIR>/steamapps/common/Rising Storm 2/Binaries/Win64"; %command%  to the launch options.

notes:
This game now seems to run better with newer versions of proton. Even the server browser is working perfectly now.
We still have to add this launch option: cd ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/"Rising Storm 2"/Binaries/Win64; %command%
But there is still one big issue: there are no servers with EAC disabled, which makes impossible to play this game online. :(

notes: It still requires command, but it finally WORKS and on all servers (EAC protected as well)!
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: The game ran fine with the command. You just have to wait a while once the you get the connection error message in order to actually load into the game. Performance wise the game ran excellent hitting a constant 60fps, however in my experience the textures seemed to be really low res despite the game being on the highest settings. I have played about with the settings but couldn't find a fix.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: "update" You have to set the streaming pool to unlimited. Despite the initial launch error the game actually runs better in Linux then Windows in my case.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes:
Using the launch option others mentioned allows the game to work. Runs with good frames but has micro-stutter issues. Easy Anti Cheat no longer kicks you so online works. Occasionally crashes after matches but is generally playable with good FPS. 

Big improvement over a couple of months ago where Anti Cheat used to kick you after a minute or so.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: The game was able to launch after I put cd "/<YOUR STEAM DIR>/steamapps/common/Rising Storm 2/Binaries/Win64"; %command% into the launch options. Then it would launch and give an error "Init failure code 3 (Curl Code: 35)", as long as you don't click ok on this error message the game will launch and run perfectly.

notes:
Problem with EAC is now fixed and the game is playable!

notes: I installed the game on a HDD. Game starts with the launch option others mentioned. Low FPS in a match and in the hub, crashes are very common. The 4.2-3 Proton version performed just as poorly as the 3.16-9 version. In a last attempt I tried disabling Esync with no real success. Maybe my hardware is to slow?
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: After placing the launch options in as stated below, (make sure you put it to your steam directory that has the game installed,) it worked perfectly.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes:
***Version 1.3 update*** I kept this command in from last time to get EAC to launch - cd "/<YOUR TEAM DIR>/steamapps/common/Rising Storm 2/Binaries/Win64"; %command%

After hitting play it started without any problems. I didn't even get the "Init failure code 3" error message, just the little splash screen and then straight into launch. I joined a server and played half a round of Saigon and another half a round of the Mamayev remake (I think it's called DMZ or similar). At all times it looked good, it sounded good, and it ran well. The leaning mechanic is amazing and very welcome to the game, I just wish they had a toggle option.

The only small issue was there seems to be an odd shadow flickering thing on all of the grass. It doesn't affect the game play at all, just looks a little odd and it was like that on my machine before the 1.3 update anyway.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: It works out of the box on Pop_OS 19.04 for my Vega 64. However, I had to turn off instanced rendering since some maps either run poorly (Hue City) or simply do not render correctly at all (Quang Tri is a visual mess with map sections either flickering or failing to render at all). Grass on vnte_dmz has an odd visual glitch as well, seemingly flickering at times. On average, it runs slower than my Windows 10 installation by 20 fps. Finally, make sure to disable vsync and the FPS limit in your video options
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: Currently works ONLINE. Performs way worse than Windows. Needs to disable Instanced Rendering in video settings to have better performance. Cranking up video settings did not negatively impact the FPS, on the contrary game started with lowest setting on first launch with bad FPS, cranking up settings and disabling framerate limits increased performances. Setting shadows to low or medium recommended to gain FPS.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes:
Runs perfectly OOTB. Multiplayer works with all servers. 

60FPS+ on all maps with high settings, SSAO, DoF, VSync, etc. I disabled "Instanced Rendering" and "Texture Streaming" and got a more stable frame rate but YMMV.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: Runs perfectly with high framerates (everything on ultra, WQHD Res). Had to disable instanced rendering in graphics settings to get rid of that weird fps cap on Hue City.

The other thing is that you need to click on the rising storm window when it launches. If you are focusing on another window, it will launch as a black screen.

notes: On Proton it works out of the box with DXVK (DX10/11 to Vulkan translation). Tested on Plasma Wayland. There are only issues with Easy Anti Cheat to play online.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes:
As for the game itself, it runs flawlessly with proton. The main problem of course still is EAC, which prevents you from playing on servers with it.

However, there is one single server right now which doesn't have it enabled (TuxFriends 32 Slots), so you technically can play online right now making the game fully playable.

Still rating it silver because it is only one server after all.
title: Rising Storm 2: Vietnam

notes: Changes as described here https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2726#issuecomment-517108154 Fixed it completely. Able to play on EAC protected servers perfectly. Huge thanks to SparrowOchon :D
